processed_queries.1000: Capitals quiz: Paris (France), Tokyo (Japan), Canberra (Australia), Brasília (Brazil), Ottawa (Canada). Quiz includes 50+ capitals.
processed_queries.1001: Trivia: The universe is 13.8 billion years old. There are an estimated 100 billion galaxies. The Milky Way is about 100,000 light-years wide.
processed_queries.1002: Did you know? The Great Wall of China is over 13,000 miles long. Cleopatra lived closer to the moon landing than the building of the pyramids.
processed_queries.1003: Science fact: Water can boil and freeze at the same time at 0.01°C. This phenomenon is called the triple point of water.
processed_queries.1004: Famous inventions timeline: 1440 - Printing Press by Gutenberg, 1876 - Telephone by Bell, 1903 - Airplane by Wright Brothers, 1971 - Microprocessor.

processed_queries.1006: Geography fact: Russia is the largest country at 17.1 million km². Canada follows at 9.98 million km². There are 195 countries worldwide.

processed_queries.1024: Existentialism: A philosophical theory emphasizing individual existence, freedom, and choice, suggesting meaning in life is self-created.
processed_queries.1025: Utilitarianism posits that actions are right if they promote happiness. Jeremy Bentham's principle of utility focuses on maximizing pleasure for the greatest number.

processed_queries.1029: Socrates employed the elenchus method, a form of cooperative argumentative dialogue, to stimulate critical thinking and illuminate ideas through questioning and refutation.
